title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A vulnerability has been found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. This issue affects some unknown...
description: A vulnerability has been found in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. This issue affects some unknown processing of the component Guestbook Plugin. Such manipulation of the argument f_text/f_slug/f_limit/f_email leads to cross site scripting. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. Upgrading to version 109.4 is capable of addressing this issue. The name of the patch is 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7. It is suggested to upgrade the affecte

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Guestbook Plugin of MaxSite CMS up to version 109.3 allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, which could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by users. This can lead to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions performed on behalf of users.
recommended_actions: Upgrade MaxSite CMS to version 109.4 or later to apply the patch (8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7).
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious XSS payloads.
Conduct a security audit of plugins and extensions to ensure no other vulnerabilities exist.

title: A weakness has been identified in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Affected by this vulnerability is an...
description: A weakness has been identified in MaxSite CMS up to 109.3. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file /admin/plugin_antispam of the component Antispam Plugin. Executing a manipulation of the argument f_logging_file can lead to cross site scripting. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading to version 109.4 addresses this issue. This patch is called 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179f

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in MaxSite CMS up to version 109.3 allows attackers to execute cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks via the Antispam Plugin. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, session hijacking, or defacement of the website.
recommended_actions: Upgrade MaxSite CMS to version 109.4 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious input targeting the Antispam Plugin.
Conduct a security audit of the CMS to ensure no other vulnerabilities are present and enable proper input validation mechanisms.

Upgrading to version 109.4 can resolve this issue. The identifier of the patch is 8a3946bd0a54bfb72a4d57179fcd253f2c550cd7. It is advisable to upgrade the af

title: A vulnerability was determined in KLiK SocialMediaWebsite up to 1.0.1. This vulnerability affects...
description: A vulnerability was determined in KLiK SocialMediaWebsite up to 1.0.1. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /includes/get_message_ajax.php of the component Private Message Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument c_id can lead to sql injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in the Private Message Handler of KLiK SocialMediaWebsite up to version 1.0.1 could allow attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to unauthorized data access, data leakage, or database compromise. This could expose sensitive user information such as private messages, user credentials, or other stored data.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'c_id' parameter in the Private Message Handler to prevent malicious SQL queries.
Update to a patched version of KLiK SocialMediaWebsite if available, or contact the vendor for a security update.
Monitor database logs for unusual activity and implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

title: A vulnerability was found in Datacom DM4100 1.3.6.1.4.1.3709. This affects an unknown part of the...
description: A vulnerability was found in Datacom DM4100 1.3.6.1.4.1.3709. This affects an unknown part of the component Ethernet Configuration Page. Performing a manipulation of the argument Name results in cross site scripting. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Datacom DM4100 Ethernet Configuration Page allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, which could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts into the web interface. This could lead to unauthorized access, data theft, or manipulation of device configurations.
recommended_actions: Apply input sanitization and validation on the 'Name' parameter to prevent malicious script injection.
Restrict access to the Ethernet Configuration Page by implementing strong authentication and network segmentation.
Monitor for any signs of exploitation and apply firmware updates or patches if the vendor releases a fix.

title: OpenClaw: Agent gateway config mutations could change protected operator settings
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

The agent-facing `gateway config.patch` / `config.apply` guard did not cover several operator-trusted settings, including sandbox policy, plugin enablement, gateway auth/TLS, hook routing, MCP server configuration, SSRF policy, and filesystem hardening. A prompt-injected model with access to the owner-only gateway tool could persist changes to those settings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw: Bundled MCP/LSP tools could bypass configured tool policy
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Bundled MCP and LSP tools could be appended to the agent's effective tool set after the normal tool-policy pipeline had already filtered core tools. If an operator configured a restrictive policy, such as a tool profile, explicit allow/deny list, owner-only tool restriction, sandbox tool policy, or subagent tool policy, a bundled MCP/LSP tool could remain ava

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw: Workspace dotenv MiniMax host override could redirect credentialed requests
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `>= 2026.4.5, < 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

A malicious workspace `.env` could set `MINIMAX_API_HOST` and redirect credentialed MiniMax requests to an attacker-controlled origin, exposing the MiniMax API key in the outbound `Authorization` header.

This requires running OpenClaw from an attacker-controlled workspace. Severity is medium.

## Fix

OpenClaw now blocks `MINIMAX_API_HOST` from

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to redirect credentialed API requests to a malicious server by manipulating the MINIMAX_API_HOST environment variable in a compromised workspace. This could result in the exposure of sensitive API keys and unauthorized access to MiniMax services.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to OpenClaw version 2026.4.20 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit and sanitize `.env` files in workspaces to ensure no malicious overrides are present.
Implement strict access controls and monitoring for workspaces to prevent unauthorized modifications.

title: OpenClaw: Browser CDP profile creation skipped strict-mode SSRF checks
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Browser profile creation normalized `cdpUrl` values before persisting them, but did not apply the configured browser SSRF policy at creation time. In deployments that explicitly disabled private-network CDP targets, a stored profile could still point at a private-network or metadata endpoint and later be probed by normal profile status flows.

Default trusted

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows bypassing strict-mode SSRF checks during browser profile creation, potentially enabling attackers to target private-network or metadata endpoints. While the severity is low due to the specific conditions required for exploitation, it could still result in unauthorized access to sensitive internal resources in affected environments.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the `openclaw` package to version 2026.4.20 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and update SSRF policies to ensure proper enforcement during all stages of browser profile creation and usage.
Conduct a security audit of existing browser profiles to identify and mitigate any potential misuse of private-network or metadata endpoints.

title: OpenClaw: Paired-device pairing actions were not limited to the caller device
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

A paired device session with limited pairing scope could enumerate global pairing state and act on pairing requests that belonged to another device within the same gateway scope ceiling.

This is a same-gateway paired-device authorization bug, not a remote unauthenticated issue. Severity is low.

## Fix

Pairing management actions are now limited to the calle

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: OpenClaw: QQBot direct media upload skipped URL SSRF validation
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

The QQBot direct-upload media path could forward attacker-controlled image URLs without applying the SSRF validation used by the local download path. This could make configured QQBot media delivery request or relay URLs the operator did not intend to allow.

The affected path is limited to QQBot outbound media handling and does not expose arbitrary local file

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: OpenClaw: MCP stdio server env could load dangerous startup variables from workspace config
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Workspace MCP stdio configuration could pass dangerous process-startup environment variables such as `NODE_OPTIONS`, `LD_PRELOAD`, or `BASH_ENV` to the spawned MCP server process. In a malicious workspace, this could make the MCP child load attacker-controlled code when the operator starts a session that uses that MCP server.

The impact is limited to local/w

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows malicious workspaces to inject attacker-controlled code into the MCP server process via dangerous environment variables, potentially leading to unauthorized code execution and compromise of local systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the `openclaw` package to version `2026.4.20` or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit workspaces and configurations for any suspicious or unauthorized environment variable settings.
Implement strict validation and sanitization of environment variables passed to MCP servers.

title: OpenClaw: Isolated cron awareness events were recorded as trusted system events
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Output from webhook-triggered isolated cron agent runs could be queued into the main session awareness stream without `trusted: false`. That made the event render as a trusted `System:` event instead of an untrusted system event.

This is a trust-labeling issue that can strengthen prompt-injection impact, but it does not directly bypass gateway auth, tool pol

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: OpenClaw: Workspace dotenv could override runtime-control environment variables
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Workspace `.env` loading did not reserve the `OPENCLAW_` runtime-control namespace broadly enough. A malicious workspace could set variables such as `OPENCLAW_GIT_DIR` before source-update or installer flows, potentially steering trusted OpenClaw runtime behavior.

This requires running OpenClaw from an attacker-controlled workspace. Severity is medium.

## F

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw: Feishu card actions could misclassify DMs and skip dmPolicy
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Feishu card-action callbacks could synthesize a message event with DM conversations classified as group conversations. That skipped `dmPolicy` enforcement for card actions, so a sender in a Feishu DM could trigger card-action flows that should have been blocked by a restrictive DM policy.

The issue is limited to Feishu card-action handling. Severity is mediu

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to bypass DM policy restrictions in Feishu card-action callbacks, potentially leading to unauthorized actions or data exposure in private conversations. This could compromise sensitive information or enable malicious activity in affected systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the `openclaw` package to version `2026.4.20` or later to apply the patch.
Review and audit existing Feishu card-action policies to ensure no unauthorized actions have occurred.
Implement monitoring and logging mechanisms to detect and respond to any suspicious card-action activity in Feishu DMs.

title: OpenClaw: Assistant media route missed scope enforcement for trusted-proxy authorization
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

The Control UI assistant-media route authenticated trusted-proxy callers but did not enforce the declared operator scopes for identity-bearing HTTP auth paths. A trusted-proxy caller without `operator.read` could access assistant-media files and metadata that were otherwise inside allowed media roots.

The route still required successful gateway authenticatio

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized access to assistant-media files and metadata through trusted-proxy calls, potentially exposing sensitive media content. However, the threat is mitigated by the requirement for successful gateway authentication.
recommended_actions: Upgrade the `openclaw` package to version `2026.4.20` or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and audit trusted-proxy configurations to ensure proper scope enforcement for sensitive routes.
Monitor access logs for unusual activity related to assistant-media routes and implement additional logging for trusted-proxy calls.

title: OpenClaw: Hook mapping templates could bypass hook session-key opt-in
description:
## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `< 2026.4.20`
- Patched version: `2026.4.20`

## Impact

Templated hook mapping `sessionKey` values were treated differently from request-supplied session keys. A hook mapping could render an externally influenced session key even when `hooks.allowRequestSessionKey` was disabled, bypassing the intended routing opt-in for hook callers.

This affects webhook routing isolation. It does not grant host execution by itse

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: CVE-2026-6576 - A vulnerability was determined in liangliangyy DjangoBlog up to 2.1.0.0. The affected element is the
description: A vulnerability was determined in liangliangyy DjangoBlog up to 2.1.0.0. The affected element is the function CommandHandler of the file servermanager/api/commonapi.py of the component WeChat Bot Interface. Executing a manipulation of the argument Source can lead to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via the WeChat Bot Interface, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of liangliangyy DjangoBlog if available, or apply temporary mitigations such as disabling the WeChat Bot Interface.
Conduct a thorough code review of servermanager/api/commonapi.py to identify and fix the command injection vulnerability in the CommandHandler function.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for user-supplied data in the Source argument to prevent exploitation.

Such manipulation of the argument key leads to hard-coded credentials. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: CVE-2026-0868 - The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cro
description: The EMC – Easily Embed Calendly Scheduling Features plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's calendly shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 4.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0868.
